_comment: SYNTHETIC TEST DATA — DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION

findings.evidence_excerpt: sbom_node::Entity.columns().add_columns(cpe::Entity.columns()).add_columns(qualified_purl::Entity.columns()) with filtering_with(query.q, q_columns()) across LEFT JOINed tables
findings.reason: Bare-value filter generates ILIKE OR across sbom_node, cpe, and qualified_purl columns from LEFT JOINed tables. PostgreSQL abandons GIN trigram index on sbom_node.name when OR spans LEFT JOINed entities, forcing sequential scan on 38 GB table.

findings.evidence_excerpt: paginate_array(&enriched, paginated.offset, paginated.limit) applied after loading all results into memory
findings.reason: Pagination parameters exist at API layer but paginate_array() applies them after loading and enriching all components into memory. All rows are fetched and processed before slicing.
Loop iterates unbounded query result (no .take() or size guard). Each iteration executes 2 DB queries (find_by_id + vendor filter). Total query count = 2N where N is unbounded.

findings.evidence_excerpt: CREATE INDEX idx_sbom_node_sbom_id_node_id ON sbom_node(sbom_id, node_id) duplicates primary key sbom_node_pkey(sbom_id, node_id)
findings.reason: Btree index idx_sbom_node_sbom_id_node_id has identical column set to sbom_node_pkey. Duplicate index wastes 52 GB disk and competes for buffer cache.

findings.evidence_excerpt: WITH RECURSIVE ancestors AS (...) on item_relates_to_item junction table with depth limit 50
findings.reason: Recursive CTE on junction table item_relates_to_item (383M rows) has depth limit 50. Depth limit exists but is generous for a junction table. Downgraded from High to Medium because depth limit prevents runaway recursion.
